name: Aaron Holiday
about: Aaron is the co-founder and Managing Partner at 645 Ventures, an early-stage venture capital firm that leads seed and Series A rounds. Aaron has 20 years of deep finance and technology expertise, having developed advanced equities Program Trading and foreign exchange derivatives software. As the first Managing Entrepreneurial Officer at Cornell Tech, Aaron worked in tandem with Greg Pass (former CTO of Twitter) to create and pioneer the campus Studio culture and pedagogy, in addition to guiding complex technology startups toward commercial success. Since founding 645 Ventures in 2014, Aaron has worked closely with portfolio company founders to strengthen and grow businesses and achieve scale in pursuit of successful M&A and IPO. 645 Ventures has invested in breakout successes, including Goldbelly, Iterable, FiscalNote, Squire, Resident/Nectar Sleep, FLY Labs (acquired by Google), Source3 (acquired by Facebook), Abacus (acquired by Certify), and several category-defining companies. Since founding 645 Ventures in 2014, Aaron has worked closely with portfolio company founders to strengthen, grow, and scale businesses. Aaron led 645 Ventures early-stage investments in several breakout successes, including Goldbelly, Iterable, FiscalNote, Squire, Resident/Nectar Sleep, FLY Labs (acquired by Google), Source3 (acquired by Facebook), and several other category-defining companies.

name: Ali Yahya
about: Ali focuses on crypto investments. Before Andreessen Horowitz, he was a software engineer and researcher at Google X, where he worked on the Everyday Robot project, which aims to build a robot that is smart and affordable enough to be viable as a consumer product for the home. He also worked at Google Brain, Googleï¿½s Artificial Intelligence lab, where he was a core developer on TensorFlow, Googleï¿½s primary library for machine learning. Ali began his career as a student and researcher at Stanford University, where he focused on the intersection of Computer Security, Distributed Systems, and Computer Networking. He first discovered the Bitcoin whitepaper in 2010 while doing research at Stanford Computer Security Lab, and has followed the space closely ever since.

name: Anamitra Banerji
about: Anamitra is part of two foundational internet companies - Overture, which invented search ads, and Twitter, which transformed communications. At Overture, Twitter - and Yahoo in between - he enjoyed the process of taking interesting technology or ideas, and transforming them into features, products - and in Twitter's case - a business.

name: Andrew Chen
about: Andrew invests in consumer technology, including social, marketplace, entertainment, and gaming experiences. Andrew is a prolific writer and leading voice on mobile, metrics, and user growth. For the past decade, heï¿½s covered the topic on andrewchen.com. He is the author of The Cold Start Problem, which explores how new start-ups launch and scale by leveraging network effects. He is also a board member and instructor at Reforge, which offers selective growth-focused programs for experienced professionals in marketing, product, data, and engineering. Prior to joining a16z, Andrew led the Rider Growth teams at Uber.

name: Ann Bordetsky
about: Serial startup operator (two unicorns, two startups exits), active angel investor and now Partner at NEA (global early stage VC with >600 tech exits). Previously Chief Operating Officer of Rival, Business Development exec at Uber, Twitter and in-the-trenches builder of two pioneering climate and mobility startups. Often helping founders with building scaling organizations, expansion and partnerships strategy, hiring 10x functional leaders and coaching on navigating team inflection points and management challenges. Angel investor in future of work, consumer, climate including Fast, Shef, Warmly, Ethena, PandoHR, Pano AI, LiveFlow, Vibe, Carbon Chain, Elpha, BeyondHQ, etc. Big believer in supporting underrepresented founders (60% of my portfolio is female founder led companies).
position: Partner; Chief Operating Officer / Business Executive for Scaling Companies

name: Anthony Zepponi
about: Anthony is an investor at Ethos VC and focuses on investing across early to late-stage businesses. He previously worked at ICONIQ Capital ï¿½ a premier growth-stage tech VC with investments including Datadog, Zoom, Robinhood, Uber, Airbnb, Snowflake, Crowdstrike and Procore amongst others.

name: Ariana Thacker
about: Chemical engineer turned investor. Partner to early-stage founders consumerizing scientific breakthrough.

name: Arianna Simpson
about: Arianna previously founded Autonomous Partners, an investment fund focused on cryptocurrencies and digital assets. She also helped launch Crystal Towers Capital, an early-stage fund investing primarily in YC companies, in 2015. Arianna previously spent time working in sales and product roles at Facebook and BitGo, an enterprise security company for bitcoin and other digital currencies.

name: Arjun Chopra
about: Arjun Chopra is a General Partner at Floodgate and leads the firm's investments in B2B and crypto. He focuses on founding teams with technical and domain insights. Before Floodgate, Arjun was the CTO at Cambridge Technologies, a publicly traded cloud computing company, where he launched and led their cloud strategy, products and services. He was a member of the board and executive team that raised multiple rounds of financing, led 350+ engineers and was recognized as one of AWS' top 20 partners world-wide. Arjun joined CTE when they acquired his Open Source startup, Vox Holdings. Arjun graduated with Special Honors and Highest Honors (summa cum laude) with a BS in CS from UT Austin. He also has an MBA from Harvard Business School, and was one of 32 nation-wide PD Soros Fellows.

name: Asad Khaliq
about: A co-founder of Acrew, Asad is excited about defragmenting data silos, applied machine learning, and enabling security & privacy. Asad is on the board of NextGen Partners, a mentorship organization supporting thousands of venture investors. He studied Management Science & Engineering at Stanford University where he also worked on turning academic research into real-world healthcare products.

name: Ash Egan
about: Ash has been leading investments in seed-stage crypto companies and protocols since 2015, having led early investments in now pillar crypto companies and protocols. Before founding Archetype, he was a Partner at Accomplice leading the firmï¿½s blockchain/crypto practice. Ash also previously co-launched the venture arm of ConsenSys and spent time at Converge VC in Boston.

name: Brayton Williams
about: Brayton has spent his career in finance working with too-big-to-fail Morgan Stanley in wealth management and then to a FinTech startup selling private company securities. Then in 2012 he joined his co-worker, Adam Draper, to start Boost VC. Brayton got rid of his apartment and moved into the dorms that house the portfolio companies as he launched the startup to fund startups. He can still be spotted heading to a room he keeps after late nights in the office. At Boost VC, Brayton wears many hats but focuses on the 300+ portfolio alumni and their needs. If those donï¿½t keep him busy enough, he also manages Boost VCï¿½s finances, general operations, social media and investment decisions.

name: Brian Ma
about: "I'm a serial entrepreneur, investor, passionate problem solver, calculated risk taker, and lifelong dream follower. Founder at Iterative Capital (http://iterative.vc/) and Founder at DivvyHomes.com (backed by a16z, GIC, Max Levchin, and Tiger Global). Previously: Founder at Weave (professional networking service, YC S14), Founder at Decide.com (ecommerce price predictions, acquired by Ebay), Zillow, Microsoft, UW Comp Sci. During non-existent spare time: random coding, motorcycle riding, board gaming, poker playing, and starcraft owning. I like to describe my life with this quote: ""You can't discover new land unless you're willing to lose sight of the shore"""

name: Brian Murray
about: Brian (aka Bmur) leads Craftï¿½s investment team. Since helping start the firm in 2017, Brian has built the infrastructure that powers the firm's investment process and has hired a talented team to expand Craft's growing portfolio. Prior to joining Craft, Brian held leadership roles at a number of B2B SaaS startups like Yammer and Zenefits. Brian is also the cofounder of Cabal, a tool that helps founders get more out of their investors and advisors.

name: Bruce Hamilton
about: Bruce Hamilton is a General Partner at Mech Ventures, a venture capital firm for pop culture, and the Founder / CEO of Wing Zero Apps (formerly known as Doly). He is passionate about using his entrepreneur experience, aesthetics, marketing and creative background to develop numerous brands (Wing Zero Apps, CrwdBoost, Grey Label Co.) and help them scale. Bruce previously served as the Chief Marketing Officer at House of Glue, an art e-commerce brand. Prior to his founding Wing Zero Apps, he was the Founder & CEO of Samurai Entertainment, a talent management firm representing artists, producers and songwriters. Bruce also worked at Epic Records as an A&R where he worked with artists such as Travis Scott, Fifth Harmony, and several others.

name: Bryan Wu
about: Bryan Wu is a partner based out of China and splits his time between Hong Kong, Beijing, and Shanghai. Prior to joining Bessemer, Bryan spent over a decade as a managing director at CPE, an alternative asset manager with an extensive presence in China. During his time at CPE, Bryan focused on investing in technology, software, and internet companies including Didi, JD Health, Ganji, Ele.me, WeBank, Bytedance, Jing-Jin Electric, AsiaInfo, Tencent Music, and many more. Bryan started his career at Startec Global Communications and has since held various positions at the intersection of technology and investing at companies like Globespan Capital Partners, McKinsey, and Legend Holdings.

name: Bucky Moore
about: Bucky is Partner at Kleiner Perkins, where he focuses on software and infrastructure investments. He is a board member and early investor in companies like Netlify, Materialize, Labelbox, Teleport, Rudderstack, Open Raven, Prisma, Calixa, and Codesandbox. Prior to entering the venture capital business in 2014, Bucky was a member of Ciscoï¿½s Corporate Development team, where he executed on various M&A, investment, and partnership opportunities on behalf of its Data Center and Cloud Business Units. Bucky holds a B.S. in Business Administration and Finance from the University of Southern California.

name: Camila Saruhashi
about: Camila is a Principal at QED Investors, a global venture capital firm specialized in fintech companies ($3B+ AUM, 150+ companies, 20+ unicorns, 13 countries). She is based in San Francisco and focuses on early stage (pre-seed, seed and Series A) investments. Prior to QED, she spent her career in fintech as an operator in fast growing companies such as Nubank and Earnin, working in key leadership roles from head of operations to product.

name: Chang Xu
about: Chang previously was a Principal at Upfront Ventures, where she focused on investing in AI and the future of work. Prior to joining venture, she was a founder and operator. She was the first Product Manager at the Minerva Project and co-founded Onion Math, an ed-tech startup in China that has raised $90M. She started her career at BCG, advising clients across tech, retail, healthcare and private equity. She holds an MBA and AB in Applied Math and Computer Science from Harvard.

name: Charlie Pinto
about: Charlie is a Principal at Bling Capital and a YCombinator alumnus. Charlie is also a Program Manager at Google and invests through his angel fund, Fundamental Ventures. Charlie is focused on SaaS, enterprise, and consumer health deals. At Google, Charlie leads operations for Google Domains and also leads a group inside the CIO organization that procures Enterprise technologies. Previously, Charlie was the co-founder and CEO of SlidePay. SlidePay was backed by YCombinator, Initialized Capital, and DCVC. SlidePay was acqui-hired by Rocket Lawyer in 2014. At Rocket Lawyer, Charlie was Director of Product Management and Director of Business Development, where he led the company into a partnership with Stripe to launch Stripe Atlas.

name: Chris Dixon
about: Chris has been at Andreessen Horowitz since 2012. He founded and is leading a16z Crypto, which invests in web3 technologies through three dedicated funds with more than $3 billion under management. Previously, Chris was the cofounder and CEO of two startups, SiteAdvisor and Hunch. SiteAdvisor was an internet security company that warned web users of security threats. The company was acquired by McAfee in 2006. Hunch was a recommendation technology company that was acquired by eBay in 2011. Chris has been a prolific seed investor, cofounding Founder Collective, a seed venture fund, and making a number of personal angel investments in various technology companies. Chris started programming as a kid, and was a professional programmer after college at the high-speed options trading firm, Arbitrade. He has a BA and MA in Philosophy from Columbia and an MBA from Harvard.

name: Chris Howard
about: Prior to founding Fuel, Chris was at Ignition Partners, where he created and ran the seed program. Previous to that, Chris spent ten years in advertising and marketing. At Heckler Associates, he launched several brands, including Aravon, New Balanceï¿½s comfort shoe business, and Procare, New Balanceï¿½s partner program.

name: Clara Brenner
about: Clara is co-founder and Managing Partner of the Urban Innovation Fund, a venture capital firm that invests in the future of cities. The fund provides seed capital and regulatory support to entrepreneurs solving our toughest urban challenges ï¿½ helping them grow into tomorrowï¿½s most valued companies. Previously, she co-founded Tumml, a startup hub for urban tech. The organization has provided 38 startups with seed funding and mentorship, and hosts thought leadership events around urban innovation. In 2014, Forbes listed her as one of its ï¿½30 Under 30ï¿½ for Social Entrepreneurship. Claraï¿½s work energizing urban entrepreneurship has been featured in numerous press outlets, including MSNBC and TechCrunch, among others. She serves on the Board of Tumml, as well as the Local Initiatives Support Corporation (LISC) Bay Area Local Advisory Committee. Clara earned her MBA from MIT Sloan and BA from NYU.

name: Clint Korver
about: Entrepreneur, Investor, Teacher, Learner

name: Courtney Chow
about: Courtney is a Vice President at Battery Ventures, a stage-agnostic venture capital firm based in San Francisco Bay Area, where she focuses on early and growth-stage consumer internet, software, and blockchain investments globally. Previously, she worked on the business-operations (growth) team at LinkedIn, where she led product strategy focused on building active communities. Prior to that, she worked on the strategy and operations team at AmazonFresh, where she focused on supply chain, inventory management and operational strategy. Courtney also spent time as a venture design fellow at IDEO, prototyping venture concepts around blockchain applications, and was also a venture analyst at Collaborative Fund, where she prospected early-stage investment opportunities.

name: Danny Brown
about: Danny Brown is a Partner at MaC Venture Capital. Prior to MaC, he was Chief of Staff at Atom Factory, an LA-based talent management firm and angel fund founded by Troy Carter. There he focused on leveraging their unique position at the intersection of media and technology on behalf of their portfolio. Simultaneously, Danny joined the Cross Culture Ventures team, where he cut his teeth in early stage venture capital while identifying the next generation of culturally impactful technologists. Danny has a wide scope of focus, geeking out on the future of digital infrastructure, emerging tech ecosystems, and creating opportunity and access to venture capital.

name: Darian Shirazi
about: One of Mark Zuckerbergï¿½s first outside hires for Facebook in 2005. Also founded the customer-data platform Radius and stayed on as its CEO for about seven years.

name: Dave Eisenberg
about: Analytics from Bain B2C startup experience: first employee & Chief of Staff at Bonobos (acq by Walmart in 2017). B2B startup experience: first business hire & Head of Product Marketing at TellApart (acq by Twitter in 2015). B2B/B2C experience: co-founder & CEO of Floored (acq. by CBRE in 2017).

name: Dave Morin
about: Dave is the Co-Founder & Managing Director of Offline Ventures. He was Founder & Partner Emeritus of Slow Ventures. He was the Co-Founder and CEO of Path which was acquired by Kakao in 2015. He co-founded Path with the mission of being a source of happiness, meaning, and connection through simplicity, quality, and privacy in technology and design. Path continues to serves tens of millions of people each month to this day.

name: Dave Munichiello
about: Dave Munichiello leads the firmï¿½s digital investing team, spanning its consumer, enterprise, and frontier practice areas. Dave is himself deeply curious about data platforms, data science, developer tools, infrastructure, and enterprise software. His investments include Bugsnag (sold to SmartBear), Cockroach, CoreOS (sold to RedHat), DeterminedAI (sold to HPE), GitLab (IPO Nasdaq: GTLB), Jet.com (sold to Walmart), Lattice.io (sold to Apple), Modular, Pixie (sold to NewRelic), Plaid, RedPanda, SambaNova, Segment (sold to Twilio), Slack (IPO, then sold to Salesforce), Snorkel, Upbound, and Wonder.

name: David George
about: Prior to joining a16z, David spent seven years at General Atlantic leading and partnering on numerous consumer internet and enterprise software growth investments, including Airbnb, AppDynamics, Opendoor, Slack, and Uber. Previously, he was an investment associate at FFL Partners and an investment banking analyst for William Blair & Company. He graduated summa cum laude from the University of Notre Dame and earned his MBA from the Stanford Graduate School of Business. He currently sits on the Advisory Council of the IDEA Center at the University of Notre Dame.

name: David Mort
about: an early-stage investor focused on entrepreneurs building the new financial economy (web3 & fintech).

name: Dillon Ferdinandi
about: Dillon joined Slack in 2016 and co-leads Slack Fund, a $100-million early-stage investment fund, and manages a global portfolio of 75+ companies. He invests in founders at the Seed and Series A stages ï¿½ specifically those with novel solutions to challenges people face in all aspects of their working lives, from collaboration and mental health to wellness, coaching, hardware, and more.

name: Dmitry Firskin
about: Dmitry joined AltaIR Capitals as a junior analyst in 2012 and became a principal then. Today AltaIR Capital manages 4 funds (of which 2 are active) with more than $600mln in assets under management. There are more than 300 companies in the fund's portfolio. AltaIR Capital is mostly focused on Fintech, Productivity SaaS, Consumer Web, HR Tech, Media, Digital Health & Wellness but experimenting with other verticals as well. Prior to AltaIR Capital Dmitry was working for the biggest provider of IT solutions and services in CIS and had entrepreneurial experience as well.

name: Dustin Rosen
about: Dustin Rosen is an investor in technology companies and the founder of Wonder Ventures. Since he began his career, heï¿½s been on every side of the business, from running and selling his own technology start-up to investing in dozens of others, as well as using this expertise to advise numerous entrepreneurs. In 2010, Dustin founded Pose, and as CEO, led the company through development, growth and three rounds of venture capital funding. In 2012, Apple named it one of the best apps of the year, and at the end of 2013, Dustin guided Pose through an acquisition before beginning Wonder Ventures. Prior to Pose, Dustin worked at the Mailroom Fund, an LA-based venture capital group comprised of the William Morris Agency, Accel Partners, Venrock, and AT&T. There, Dustin focused on discovering and funding high-impact and innovative companies in the internet consumer arena. Before this, he worked at the William Morris Agency, getting his start in the infamous Mail Room.

name: Ed Sim
about: Ed is a seasoned VC with over 25 years of experience leading first-round investments and a passion for working with developer-first, engineering-driven founders. He founded boldstart in 2010 and has served as a day-one partner and board member or observer for cutting-edge tech names including Common Paper, Env0, and Spectro Cloud.

name: Elizabeth Yin
about: Elizabeth Yin is a co-founder and General Partner at Hustle Fund, a pre-seed fund for software entrepreneurs. Previously, Elizabeth was a partner at 500 Startups where she invested in seed stage companies and ran the Mountain View accelerator. In a prior life, Elizabeth co-founded and ran an adtech company called LaunchBit (acq 2014). Elizabeth has a BSEE from Stanford and an MBA from MIT Sloan. Former adtech entrepreneur. Former accelerator manager. Mostly can help w/ fundraising strategy / networking / blurb + deck.

name: Erik Torenberg
about: Co-CEO, On Deck. Community builder, company founder, and startup investor.

name: Ethan Kurzweil
about: Ethan is a partner in the San Francisco office and a leading investor in a variety of technology verticals. Heï¿½s passionate about new platforms that provide technologists and business users with democratized access to cutting-edge technical tooling. Before joining Bessemer in 2008, he worked for Dow Jones & Co., managing the turnaround of international editions of the Wall Street Journal, and Linden Lab, the creator of Second Life. Earlier in his career, Ethan co-founded SSB-Bart Group (now known as Level-Access), a company whose mission is to make the Internet accessible to users with disabilities.

name: Eva Alonso
about: Prior to Ribbit Capital, Eva served as an investor at Goldman Sachs

name: Evan Moore
about: Evan is a GP at Khosla, and previously on the founding teams of Opendoor, DoorDash and Vevo.

name: Fabrice Grinda
about: Formerly Co-Founder and Co-CEO of OLX, the largest classifieds site in Russia, Brazil, India, Pakistan, Poland, Ukraine, Romania, Portugal and many other markets. Prior to OLX, he was the CEO and founder of Zingy, a mobile content company. Previously worked as a management consultant for McKinsey Holds a BA in Economics from Princeton

name: Fady Yacoub
about: Co-Founder and Managing Partner at HOF Capital. Focuses on companies leveraging frontier technologies to reshape massive industries. At HOF, he led investments in Epic Games, Klarna, Flutterwave, Yoco, SoFi, and others across sectors and stages.

name: Fang Yuan
about: Formerly of Baidu Ventures, where she led BVï¿½s investment in Covariant.

name: Gary Sheynkman
about: Gary is the co-founder and GP of Leyden Ventures. Gary lives in Dubai and works across early stage venture and public sectors. Prior to launching Leyden Ventures, Gary led the launch and day-to-day management of Techstars Dubai (ï¿½TSDXBï¿½). He is the host of ??? TacoStars, the largest founder social community in the UAE. Prior to launching TSDXB, Gary was consulting with The Executive Office of His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice President and Prime Minister of the UAE and Ruler of Dubai (ï¿½TEOï¿½) on development of Area 2071, a new business cluster within Emirates Towers focused on creative collisions between startups and large corporates.

name: Gautam Gupta
about: Prior to co-founding A-Star, Gautam served as the COO/CFO at Opendoor, and as Head of Finance at Uber. He also served as a Vice President at Goldman Sachs.

name: Geri Kirilova
about: Geri Kirilova is a Partner at Laconia where she leads seed investments in business-to-business software companies. Prior to joining Laconia, Geri was a Business Associate at Techstars, where she facilitated program operations and worked directly with ten portfolio companies on customer acquisition, growth strategy, fundraising, business development, and messaging and storytelling. Geri began her venture capital career in Central and Eastern Europe at Launchub Ventures in Sofia and Credo Ventures in Prague. Leads pre-seed and seed investments in b2b software companies, predominantly HQ'd in the US & Canada. Previously at Techstars IoT (NYC), LAUNCHub (Sofia), and Credo Ventures (Prague).

name: Grace Isford
about: Grace is the youngest principal and investment team member at Canvas Ventures, which she first joined part-time in 2016 as a campus scout while still studying at Stanford. Since joining full-time in 2019, she has been promoted 3 times. Grace has sourced 10 deals representing over $100M in deployed capital for Canvas including Robocorp, Skyflow, Vendia and Flowspace and serves as either a board director or observer on seven of those ten companies. Prior to Canvas, Grace worked on the LP side at the Stanford Management Company and in product at edtech startup Handshake. Sheï¿½s passionate about increasing female representation in venture and tech - she served as President of Stanford Women in Business and as an All Raise Captain. Grace also serves on the board of Stanford Technology Ventures Program, which stems from her participation in the Mayfield Fellowship program. invests at the nexus of web3, data infrastructure, and applications of AI/ML especially in healthcare and financial services. focuses on crypto and blockchain infrastructure companies building the next-gen web3 stack as well as data and machine-learning startups that are hyper-personalizing user experiences and transforming legacy industries. Before joining Lux, Grace was a principal at Canvas Ventures Prior to Canvas, Grace worked on the LP side at the Stanford Management Company

name: Herston Powers
about: Herston is a Co-founder & Managing Partner at 1982 Ventures. Prior to founding 1982 Ventures, he was a Principal at tryb Group in Singapore and created and executed their early stage investment strategy.

name: Ilya Eremeev
about: Video games industry professional with over 15 years of experience (10 in development and 5 in investments) focused on free-to-play games. General Partner in the VC fund, scouting for promising games companies and helping them grow.

name: James Currier
about: 5-time founder. Expert on network effects.

name: Jamie Goldstein
about: Serves as Chairman of Petri, a program co-founded by Pillar VC focused on supporting pre-seed companies attacking the worldï¿½s largest problems at the intersection of computation, biology and engineering. Prior to Pillar, Jamie was an investing partner for 18 years at North Bridge Venture Partners.

name: Jamie Quint
about: Jamie is a General Partner at Uncommon Capital. He has 15 years of experience as one of the foremost product growth practitioners in Silicon Valley. He is an engineer by training, and started his career as a startup founder, joining one of the first few Y Combinator classes in 2008. Since then, he has consulted on growth at breakout companies such as Twitch, Everlane, Mercury, and Substack. He has worked as a senior executive at Notion and Reddit, leading both growth and revenue. Jamie helps portfolio companies with product focused growth, product strategy, product hiring, data infrastructure, performance marketing, lifecycle marketing, and more.

name: Jason Calacanis
about: Host of This Week in Startups and the All In podcast. Investor in over 300 startups.

name: Jason Shuman
about: Jason Shuman is a Partner at Primary, where he leads new investments in consumer healthcare, fintech, proptech and marketplaces. The Boston native and University of Miami alum has jumped between sides of the table over the last eight years but started his career as the Founder of a D2C footwear company. Forbes featured him on the 30 Under 30 in Venture Capital list; Venture Capital Journal named him one of Venture Capital's 40 rising stars. Founder turned investor. On a mission to help empower others to have the confidence and tools to live a more fulfilling, successful life, however they define it.

name: Jason Warner
about: Previously CTO at Github, Heroku, and Canonical.

name: Jed Lenzner
about: Jed Lenzner is a Vice President at Tusk Venture Partners where he is involved in all aspects of the firmï¿½s venture capital investment practice. Prior to Tusk Venture Partners, Jed led Strategy and Corporate Development at ASAPP, a software company focused on developing AI-Native products for customer experience teams. During that time, he was involved in the firmï¿½s go-to-market strategy and finance operations and was responsible for strategic partnerships. Before joining ASAPP, Jed spent eight years at Allen & Company, the merchant bank, where he was involved in all phases of M&A and financing transactions for enterprise software and consumer internet businesses. He had the opportunity to work closely with the management teams at companies such as Twilio, Okta, Zuora, New Relic, Anaplan, Domo, Desire2Learn, Bonobos, Bedrock Manufacturing, Centene, among others.

name: Jenieri Cyrus
about: Jenieri is an investor with Urban Innovation Fund.

name: Joe Tsai
about: Joe focuses on early stage investments, particularly in software, fintech and marketplaces. Previously, Joe was a Vice President on the investment team at Stripes, where he invested in companies including On Running (NYSE: ONON), BacklotCars (acquired by KAR), Cypress.io, Emotive, Pleo, and Sift. Prior to Stripes, Joe was a management consultant in McKinsey's business technology practice, where he advised Fortune 500 clients on digital strategy and transformation topics including eCommerce enablement, online customer journeys, and data analytics and IT infrastructure. Earlier, Joe was one of the first research analysts at CB Insights, where he delved into the industry of startups and venture capital while working within what was then a ~10 person startup itself. A graduate of the University of Pennsylvania's Jerome Fisher Program in Management and Technology, Joe holds a BS in Economics from the Wharton School and a BAS in Computer Science from Penn Engineering.

name: John Prendergass
firm_name: Ben Franklin Technology Partners of Southeastern PA

name: John Smothers
about: John is an investor at Acrew. Prior to joining Acrew, John co-founded an eCommerce focused startup studio where he helped successfully bootstrap multiple brands. In addition to his work in the CPG industry, John co-founded Dimension Ventures, an early-stage angel fund. As an angel, he has invested in companies like Brew.com, Bito, Finix, Pipe, Hologram, Prenda, Rosebud.ai, Sorare, and others. John serves on the comms committee at BLCK VC and co-founded a restaurant development company. He strongly believes hospitality principles can carry over to the tech ecosystem. John attended the University of Delaware where he studied leadership and business in the Biden School of Public Policy and Horn Entrepreneurship program. A two-time entrepreneur who has also backed 30+ companies as an angel and institutional investor, John joined Scribble Ventures to return to his roots of partnering with founders at the earliest stages.

name: John Vrionis
about: A venture capitalist since 2006, John has been an early investor in over a dozen billion dollar plus companies, including: Affirmed Networks, AppDynamics, Arctic Wolf Networks, Carta, Harness, Liftoff.io, Mulesoft, Nicira, Nimble Storage, Sourcegraph, and DataStax. Inspired by successful entrepreneurs, John arrived in Silicon Valley in 2002 and worked in product management before completing his MBA at Stanford. John looks to work with entrepreneurs who have the hunger to learn and the drive to overcome difficult obstacles. In Johnï¿½s words, ï¿½I believe that you learn by overcoming adversity. For me, whether it was sports, school, or in my personal and professional life, thatï¿½s been the truth. I gravitate to people who have an immense drive and the humility to keep learning.ï¿½ Prior to founding Unusual Ventures, John was a General Partner at Lightspeed Venture Partners.

name: Johnnie Yu
about: Johnnie is a GenZ investor at Listen, an early-stage consumer VC fund behind brands like Dame, Calm, Public Goods, Factor, KiwiCo, Interior Define, and more. While obsessed with all things consumer, he is particularly interested in Web3 and brands that build at the intersection of art/culture and commerce. He is also the founder and a core team member at ConsumerDAO, a community of professional consumer founders, investors, and brand geeks passionate about bridging Consumer + Web3.

name: Jon Broscious
about: Prior to joining Mucker Capital, Jon spent 3.5 years at Social Starts and Joyance Partners focusing on software investments in alternative education, consumer brands and healthcare, with a particular interest in new models of distribution across patients, insurers, and providers. Before his time with Social Starts, Jon was a Director with the Advisory Board Company (acquired by Optum) and Education Advisory Board (acquired by Vista) on its internal Strategy and Operations team focusing on revenue maximization across new sales and renewals. He guided teams on new product launches, staffing optimization, market research, customer acquisition and retention, and worked with the corporate development team on several acquisitions within the healthcare provider and higher education industries. He also spent time on revenue cycle consulting with health systems across the US.

name: Jorge Conde
about: Jorge has more than 20 years of experience in leading, building, operating, and investing in life sciences and healthcare, with a broad range of operational roles in early-stage and commercial companies. Prior to joining a16z, Jorge served as Chief Strategy Officer, Chief Financial Officer, and Chief Product Officer for Syros (NASDAQ: SYRS), which is advancing small molecules to control the expression of genes. Jorge was also co-founder and CEO of Knome, a human genome interpretation company acquired by Tute Genomics in 2015.

name: Josh Berns
about: Josh is a generalist investor at Blue Collective based in NYC. Previously, he founded and led a venture-backed startup in the food-tech space.

name: Julie Lein
about: "Julie is a co-founder and Managing Partner of the Urban Innovation Fund, a venture capital firm that invests in the future of cities. Previously, she co-founded Tumml, a startup hub for urban tech. In 2016, Living Cities named her as one of its ""25 Disruptive Leaders."" Julie's work energizing urban-focused entrepreneurship has been featured in numerous press outlets, including CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle, among others. She serves on the Boards of Tumml and Empower Work."

name: Jyoti Bansal
about: Multi-unicorn founder. founded AppDynamics in 2008 acquired by Cisco last 2017. CEO and Co-Founder of Harness Forbesï¿½ ï¿½Best Cloud Computing CEO to Work Forï¿½, and ï¿½Best CEOï¿½ by San Francisco Business Times at the Annual Tech & Innovation Award. named Ernst & Young Entrepreneur Of The Yearï¿½ for Northern California in 2016.

name: Karl Alomar
about: Karl Alomar is a serial entrepreneur turned investor with extensive experience in starting, developing and selling high growth businesses in various market segments. Karl co-founded two successful ventures of his own, followed by 6 years as COO at DigitalOcean helping build the company from ground up to $250MM ARR.

name: Kevin Colleran
about: Kevin Colleran is the Co-Founder and Managing Director at Slow Ventures.

name: Lan Xuezhao
about: Founder of Basis Set, the largest seed fund raised by a solo GP in 2017. Lan built and led the Corporate Development Strategy team at Dropbox during its most acquisitive time. She previously managed teams at McKinsey in New York and Shanghai, advising Fortune 500 companies on issues including big data, AI, and growth strategy. Earlier in her career, Lan designed and developed brain training games as a founder and studied human brain functions for her Ph.D. She also received her M.A. in Statistics, both from the University of Michigan.

name: Lauren Kolodny
about: "Focuses on investments in what Acrew has coined ï¿½financial services rebuiltï¿½ and ï¿½work reimagined"" Prior to Acrew she was a partner at Aspect Ventures, which she joined at its founding and where she helped to build out the team. Before becoming an investor, she worked in product marketing at Google, where she led a number of launches for GSuite, including Google Drive Began career building tech and finance partnerships for the Clinton Foundation in India Lauren received a BA in international relations from Brown University, an MSc from Stanford University, and an MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business"

name: Lex Zhao
about: At One Way Ventures, Lex identifies investment opportunities, leads diligence, and works with entrepreneurs to help build large, enduring companies. Lex previously led the operations team at Vestwell, a venture-backed fintech startup. Prior to Vestwell, Lex earned his MBA with Honors from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business. At Chicago Booth, he was an Associate at Moderne Ventures and took part in the New Venture Challenge startup accelerator. Lex started his career at Bridgewater Associates, the worldï¿½s largest hedge fund, and NERA Economic Consulting, a leading economic consultancy. Lex graduated Phi Beta Kappa from Cornell University where he earned his B.A. in Economics with Distinction. Lex has also passed all three levels of the CFA Program. Location: Boston and New York

name: Mario Mitchell
about: Mario Mitchell is a natural business developer, marketer, and chief operator. Having served in the military, Mario learned how to truly become a leader while perfecting his skills at design thinking and relationship management. Mario believes in a collaborative consultative approach and commitment to exceeding expectations. A firm believer that if given the chance people can create greatness. Everyone has a dream and just needs a helping hand. Working with nonprofits in Las Vegas like Nevada Partners Promised Neighborhood, Mario helps create technology programs to enhance the Westside community. Mario serves as the lead Ambassador for Bunker Labs in Las Vegas and is the Nevada State Coordinator for Global Entrepreneurship Network. He is also the Chairman/ Vice President of the Southern Nevada Veteran Chamber of Commerce. Looking to create a sustainable ecosystem for entrepreneurs in Las Vegas. Heï¿½s also a tech advocate, who fully supports Blacks and Veterans in technology.

name: Martin Casado
about: Martin was previously the cofounder and chief technology officer at Nicira, which was acquired by VMware for $1.26 billion in 2012. While at VMware, Martin was a fellow, and served as senior vice president and general manager of the Networking and Security Business Unit, which he scaled to a $600 million run-rate business by the time he left VMware in 2016. He started his career at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory where he worked on large-scale simulations for the Department of Defense before moving over to work with the intelligence community on networking and cybersecurity. These experiences inspired his work at Stanford where he created the software-defined networking (SDN) movement, leading to a new paradigm of network virtualization. While at Stanford he also cofounded Illuminics Systems, an IP analytics company, which was acquired by Quova Inc. in 2006.

name: Matt Brezina
about: Matt Brezina founded, scaled & sold two startups: Xobni YC S06 (acq Yahoo), Sincerely (acq Liberty Media). Matt has been angel investing since 2008 and has backed over 80 companies including breakouts: Dropbox, Ring, FunctionOfBeauty, Solugen, Mercury, Snapdocs, Cruise, Rescale, Tonal, Karat. Matt has invested across a diverse range of markets including Marketplaces, HealthTech, B2B SAAS, Fintech, Hardware+service, Climate Tech as well as across the US, Latam, Africa, Middle East & Europe. Matt was one of the earliest scouts, deploying money for CRV & Spark Capital. Matt is hyper-involved in hiring, fundraising, positioning and product from day zero through series A, and then shifts to a founder advisory role. His goal is to be the first person founders call for advice. Matt has helped on over 12 M&A transactions. Matt also started the Penn State Summer Founders Program and is the Founder of KidSafeSF a movement to make streets safe for travel by walking & bicycle.

name: Matt Fates
about: Mattï¿½s passion for new ideas and technology started at a young age, dating back to coding with an Apple IIe.  Professionally, he was drawn to venture capital as it provides the opportunity to work closely with entrepreneurs leveraging technology to solve challenging problems.  Today, he values the opportunity to be a partner to founders, developing strong relationships, reasoning through strategic issues, and supporting them in building impactful companies. Matt has worked with early stage technology driven companies since 1996, from formation stage all the way through IPO and beyond through different roles. As a venture investor and angel, either through his firm or individually, Matt has invested in over 100 companies.

name: Matt Heiman
about: Matt Heiman is a partner and co-founder of Vetamer Capital, an investment firm that makes concentrated long-term investments in both public and private companies globally, with a focus on financial technology and related sectors. Previously he served as a Partner at CRV and an investor at Greylock Partners. Earlier in his career he worked at McKinsey & Co.

name: Matthew Garratt
about: Matt Garratt currently serves as the Managing Partner of Salesforce Ventures, the strategic investment arm for Salesforce with over 275 portfolio companies across 17 countries. While at Salesforce, Matt has completed over 50 Investments and Acquisitions in leading enterprise SaaS companies includingcompanies such as DocuSign, Mulesoft and Twilio.

name: Megan Ananian
about: Megan is GP at The Helm, a NYC-based women-led fund investing in female founders. Prior to joining the team, Megan invested in early-stage startups at Seedinvest, worked as a product manager at places like Circle Pay and 2U, and financed over $1Bn in bond transactions at Wells Fargo. She received her MBA from Yale and BS from University of South Carolina.

name: Michael Bloch
about: Michael is a Partner at Quiet Capital, where he leads early-stage investments in crypto/web3 & fintech startups. He is a former founder, operator, and angel investor in over 80 companies. Previously, Michael founded Pillar, a fintech company that was acquired by Acorns. Before Pillar, Michael was one of the first 100 employees at DoorDash, where he led the California & New York regions and grew them from $5M to $100M in revenue.

name: Mitra Lohrasbpour
about: Joined SPC from Nava Public Benefit Corporation, the software company responsible for the improved healthcare.gov insurance marketplace. At Nava, Mitra spearheaded business development efforts and won contracts also to build new VA benefit appeals and Medicare incentive payments systems. Prior to Nava, she led revenue analytics and international expansion efforts at Dropbox. Mitra began her career at Bain as a management consultant and is on the board of LeanMarket. She graduated from MIT.

name: Morgan Beller
about: Co-founder of Libra and was Head of Strategy for Novi, Facebookï¿½s digital wallet for the Libra payment system. Originally joined Facebook as part of the Corporate Development team in 2017 where she worked on defining Facebookï¿½s strategy around blockchain, cryptocurrencies, and decentralized technology. Prior to Facebook, Morgan ran Corporate Development at Medium where she led the Embedly acquisition and played a key role in developing Mediumï¿½s subscription strategy. She began her career on the Deal Team at Andreessen Horowitz.

name: Morgan Livermore
about: Morgan Livermore is a Partner at Quiet Capital and focuses on emerging enterprise applications, DevOps, IT Infrastructure, fintech, and has a passion for autonomous vehicle technologies. Prior to Quiet, Morgan was a Partner at Geodesic Capital and involved with the investments in JFrog, Aurora, Marqeta, UiPath, Imply.io, Workato, HashiCorp, Vercel, Confluent, Clearcover, and MX. Prior to Geodesic, Morgan was an investor at Accel, where he invested in GoFundMe, PagerDuty, Algolia, and Browserstack. Prior to Accel, Morgan was an Associate at Vista Point Advisors. He was previously the owner of Evolving Vox. Morgan lives in San Francisco with his wife, Caroline. Heï¿½s an avid Bay Area sports fan and plays goalie for the Olympic Club Menï¿½s Soccer Team.

name: Nakul Mandan
about: I am the Founder at Audacious Ventures, where we lead seed stage investments. Prior to Audacious, I was a partner at Lightspeed. Over the last decade, I've backed several category defining companies and marketplaces such as Marketo, Gainsight, People.ai, 6Sense, BuildingConnected, WorkOS, Multiverse amongst others. I look for ambitious and hungry product founders targeting large markets. I believe hunger and ambition are more important ingredients of success than experience.

name: Neil Devani
about: Neil invests in companies that improve people's lives. He primarily focuses on companies using deep technologies and those in the healthcare, financial services, and education sectors. Neil is also an advisor to Upsolve, a bankruptcy nonprofit that has helped individuals clear hundreds of millions of dollars of primarily medical debt, and previously to Copia, a venture-backed technology company that has delivered over three million meals to people in need.

name: Nicholas Chirls
about: Nick manages seed investing for betaworks. Previously, he worked at HappyFunCorp, lived in China, and briefly traded commercial jet engines at Lehman Brothers.

name: Nick Grossman
about: Previously led an incubator for startups at the intersection of cities and data at OpenPlans, with a focus on cultivating open source communities, building open data platforms, and supporting open standards efforts.

name: Nick Moran
about: Prior to founding New Stack, Nick began his career as a Developer. He went on to work in M&A and Product Management for Danaher. There he developed one of the most successful products in the company's history -an analytical device for testing compounds in drinking water. In May 2014, Nick founded the 1st VC podcast, The Full Ratchet, which has grown to over 90k active subscribers.

name: Nick Pappageorge
about: Nick is on the research and ventures teams at Delphi Digital. Nick has a focus on DeFi, but invests across crypto sectors. Previously, Nick was a longtime analyst at CB Insights, where he covered crypto, fintech, and payments.

name: Nick Shalek
about: Earlier in his career, Nick was a Director at Verne Global and a Senior Analyst at Yale Investments, where he contributed to over $800 million in new investment commitments across a diversity of asset classes, including U.S. public equities, venture capital, and natural resources. Extending his energy to service in the public sector, Nick was also elected to New Havenï¿½s Board of Alderman ï¿½the city councilï¿½ where he focused his efforts on the cityï¿½s economic development and education policy.

name: Nicole Williams
about: Nicole is an investor at an early stage, thesis driven, frontier tech fund called Compound. She invests in all areas of frontier tech but is focused on digital health and personalized medicine, machine learning & federated learning, lab automation, game engines, and near term hybrid quantum classical algorithms and quantum sensing. Previously she worked with a startup running experimental educational initiatives to create hands-on learning experiences for under-represented groups to cutting edge STEM domains with organizations like NASA, DARPA, and the NIH; studied data science and worked at Capterra, a Gartner company. Nicole is based in NYC, and invests globally.

name: Pano Anthos
about: Pano founded XRC Labs in 2015 and is the companyï¿½s Managing Director. Prior to this, he served in a number of leadership roles for media, retail, consumer goods and industrial companies. He also founded four digital media and software startups, including Gather Education, Hangout Industries which was the first to bring video game technology and name brands to Facebook in 2007, Pantero and Syntra / ClearCross. He began his career in supply chain and trademarked the term ï¿½global logistics systems.ï¿½ He received his BA from the University of Delaware and SIA from Columbia University.

name: Parul Singh
about: Parul Singh is a product-driven founder and investor with a background in engineering, product strategy, and analytics. Parul spent the past five years on the investing team at Founder Collective, a tech-focused, early-stage seed fund where she covered Boston and NYC, evaluated over 3,000 companies, and spearheaded the firmï¿½s investments in Embark Veterinary, Broadlume, Running Tide, HumanFirst, Flume Health, and HealthNote. Prior to investing, she spent close to a decade as an engineer and product manager at the NYTimes.com, Inc. and Fast Company, and she was the founder of a learning analytics company. At Initialized, Parul will focus her investing efforts on software companies, with a special interest in the areas of healthtech, SaaS, and analytics. Parul has an undergraduate degree from Harvard College and an MBA from MIT.

name: Patrick Harmon
about: Investor at B Capital Group Ascent Fund in LA. Passionate about partnering with talented founders addressing disruptive macro trends. Focus on vertical SaaS and b2b marketplaces. Bias for vertical solutions in massive, fragmented, legacy industries with low NPS, or B2B/B2B2C solutions with B2C virality. Previous investor at Morpheus Ventures, BMW i Ventures and Dalus Capital.

name: Paul Levine
about: Paul is a General Partner at Sapphire Ventures and leads investments in fintech, SMB/vertical SaaS, proptech, and marketplaces. He brings 20 years of experience as an operator and leader of high-growth organizations including Trulia, Zillow Group, Yahoo!, and E*TRADE. Paul is a board member at Restream and Side. He led Sapphire Venturesï¿½ investments in AvidXchange, Mercury, and Tonal, and is active with investments in Current and Podium. Prior to joining Sapphire, Paul was president and COO of Trulia, where he helped lead Truliaï¿½s transformation from a private 100-person company with $20 million in revenue, to a successful standalone public company and to its eventual merger with Zillow Group (NASDAQ: ZG) to create a company with more than 3,000 employees, $1 billion in revenue and $10 billion in market capitalization.

name: Peter Levine
about: Peter was previously senior vice president and general manager of the Data Center and Cloud Division at Citrix where he was responsible for revenue, product management, business development, and strategic direction. Peter joined Citrix in 2007 through its $500 million acquisition of XenSource, the leading provider of enterprise-class open source virtualization software, where he served as CEO.

name: Phil Boyer
about: focuses on investments in enterprise applications, infrastructure, vertical software, and frontier technology. passionate about serving as a partner with early-stage founding teams, with an eye towards helping strong technical teams build category-defining companies. Prior to Crosslink, Phil was an investor with NYC-based venture capital firm Tenfore Holdings  Before beginning his venture capital career, Phil wrote and published research on the technology sector at Credit Suisse and RBC

name: Phil Brady
about: previously on the deal team at Andreessen Horowitz, he focused on IoT and crypto. spent time working at tech startups such as Airware and Xapo, as well as PE/VC firms such as Lindsay Goldberg in NY and Inventure Oy in Finland.

name: Rahul Prakash
about: Rahul is a successful 3x technology entrepreneur turned venture capitalist. The last company he co-founded, MyEnergy, sold to Nest prior to Google's $3.2B acquisition. His previous company sold to NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) for $120MM. His first venture backed company, Spock, sold to Intelius in 2009. In 2013, he founded NOMO Ventures, a venture capital fund with $115 million in assets under management. In a previous life, he served as a Press Secretary to a number of city & statewide elected officials and also had a stint in the music industry with Columbia/RuffHouse Records. He also loves hip hop, hoops, and single malt.

name: Ramtin Naimi
about: Ramtin is the founder and general partner of Abstract Ventures.

name: Raviraj Jain
about: Joined Lightspeed in 2017. focuses on enterprise IT, cloud-based services and applications, and machine intelligence

name: Raymond Tonsing
about: Raymond Tonsing is a San Francisco based technology investor and entrepreneur. He is the founder and managing partner of Caffeinated Capital, a technology investment firm with a portfolio that includes Affirm, Airtable, Brex, Color, dYdX, Opendoor, Virta, and Wish. Previously, Mr. Tonsing was a partner at Akkadian Ventures, a secondary fund focused on providing liquidity to founders, early employees, and angel investors of private, high-growth technology companies. Before that, Mr. Tonsing helped develop and manage Energy Central and Rigzone, two market-leading technology platforms that provide information on the energy industry. Mr. Tonsing received his B.A. from the University of Colorado, Boulder.

name: Richard Dulude
about: Co-Founder & Partner at Underscore VC, a US-based venture firm focused on enterprise software from the earliest stages (pre-seed, seed, series A). With $250M AUM to date, across three funds, we have built only 22 world class LP relationships with institutions, endowments, family offices, and strategic individuals. I am also personally an LP into many early stage managers in Europe with intent to increase that portfolio.

name: Rico Mallozzi
about: Rico is a Vice President at Sapphire Sport where he leads and sources investment opportunities across the media, crypto, entertainment, and consumer software landscape. Rico is particularly passionate about virtual worlds and decentralized technology. He oversees Sapphire Sportï¿½s unique platform of investors, networks, and communities to help drive tangible results to Sportï¿½s portfolio. Rico has actively worked on investments in FalconX, Tonal, Flowhaven, Manticore, and Buzzer. Rico is a board observer at Flowhaven. Ricoï¿½s experience in the technology industry spans across multiple functional areas. He began his career working at start-ups in the renewable energy industry and more recently held positions at Cisco and SAP. Rico has also supported entrepreneurs in Indonesia and Sri Lanka in developing business models for social venture funds. Rico has lived and worked in Italy, Indonesia, Sri Lanka, Singapore, and Thailand.

name: Russ Wilcox
about: Russ was a founder and CEO of E Ink, which commercialized electronic paper displays invented at MIT. The company reached $200 million in revenue and was acquired for half a billion dollars in 2009. He also started and led university spin-outs related to energy and drug discovery. Russ invests in companies enabled by data, science, and technology and applied to a wide range of industries including proptech, computing, biotechnology, consumer, hardware, and blockchain gaming.

name: Ryan Freedman
about: Ryan Freedman is an entrepreneur turned investor on a never-ending journey to bring efficiency and innovation to the real world. Prior to Alpaca, Ryan founded Corigin, a real estate investment firm with $600 million AUM. He led this firm with a lens towards innovation and has been at the forefront of analyzing and adopting real estate technology to enhance operations and investment decisions. To that end, Corigin was an early pioneer of the co-living concept, infusing experience and community into multi-family long before others thought to. In 2008, Ryan co-founded Coral Capital, a tech-enabled commercial finance operation that has provided over 1.5 billion in financing to small businesses. His obsession with technology and efficiency informed his natural progression from a real estate executive to a venture capitalist and PropTech investor.
position: General Partner, Founder, Chairman & Chief Executive Officer

name: Sam Campbell
about: Sam is an investor at Samsung Next, an early-stage corporate venture arm of Samsung Electronics, where he focuses on crypto & fintech companies. Prior to joining Next, Sam was managing direct venture investments for Stanford University. Sam has actively partnered with companies at the earliest stages, including jumping in part-time to build in the trenches with founders. Sam resides in the SF Bay Area with his growing family.

name: Sergio Romo
about: As CEO of Grin, I helped to grow the company from $0 to over $40M USD in annual revenue and the team from 4 to 2000 people with operations in 7 countries (Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Chile, Argentina, Peru and Uruguay) and 22 cities (Sao Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Mexico City, Lima, Bogota, Buenos Aires, Santiago et al) in less than two years. Sergio also co-founded Investo, an investment firm to invest in companies in early stage startups, leading seed investments in Rappi, Solugen, Mercury, Justo, Ripio, Cambly, Unbabel and 70+ more startups like Nowports, Beek, Casai, BillPocket, YoTePresto, 100 Ladrillos, Eva and Runa. Sergio Romo co-founded Miorden.com in 2010. In 2012 SinDelantal.com bought MiOrden.com who later sold to JustEat to become the leader in Mexico in food delivery from 2013-2016. Sergio has been a mentor to over a 100 technology startups and writes essays about entrepreneurship at www.medium.com/@romo

name: Shiyan Koh
about: Operator (@Nerdwallet) turned Pre-seed investor (@hustlefundvc); Mom of 2, thrower of dinner parties, lover of serendipity

name: Sigal Mandelker
about: Has held numerous high-ranking positions at the U.S Department of Treasury, the U.S. Department of Justice, and the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. Sigal was also a Partner at Proskauer LLP, served as a law clerk on the US Supreme Court, and worked on Capitol Hill.

name: Soraya Darabi
about: "Soraya is a founder of TMV, a venture firm investing in startups reimagining the future. TMV backs companies focused on ideas that will reshape industries or inspire new ones. Her investments include: Figs, Casper, Cityblock, Hungryroot, Kindbody, Parsley Health, Ridwell, Gimlet, Clockwise, Running Tide, Bulletin. Soraya began her career as Manager of Social Media at The New York Times. She went on to co-found the application Foodspotting, named by Apple as an ï¿½App of the Year"" (acquired by OpenTable). Soraya has been featured on the cover of Fast Company magazine's ï¿½100 Most Creative People in Businessï¿½ issue and on the cover of Brand Week. She was named a Fortune Magazine ""40 Under 40"" (2015). She sat on the digital advisory board of GE, is a WEF ""Young Global Leader,"" a 6-time mentor to TechStars New York - 3-times ""Mentor of the Year."" Soraya graduated magna cum lade from Georgetown University, completed the Global Leadership and Public Policy module at Harvard Kennedy School."

name: Tom Drummond
about: Tom is the cofounder and first investing partner at Heavybit. Heï¿½s spent the last 15 years working with developer tool, SaaS and enterprise infrastructure teams and helps founders with their early operations and financial reporting, fundraising and GTM.

name: Trevor Zimmerman
about:
"Previously, he co-founded Farmore Capital Group (FCG), a private investment firm. While there, he led the firmï¿½s day-to-day activities and spent much of his time working directly with entrepreneurs to build successful companies.

In addition to angel investing and venture capital, Trevorï¿½s professional experience ranges from developing materials for biomedical implants to designing high-performance batteries for the U.S. Navy to developing systems to hinder the migration of radionuclides in groundwater at the Y-12 National Security Complex. Before co-founding FCG, Trevor was the principal engineer in the Technology Development Division of Kurion Inc, a venture-backed startup company. Here he was primarily focused on radioactive wastewater treatment efforts at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant. Trevor holds patents for making carbon nanopipes and ductwork with nanomeric walls and separating radionuclides with submersible filters.

Trevor frequently serves as an instructor, mentor, and judge for a variety of institutions including the Department of Energy, Stanford University, and MIT. He completed a postgraduate research appointment at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, and holds a M.S. in Environmental Engineering from Clemson University and a B.A. in Chemistry from Alfred University."

name: Tripp Jones
about: I am a Partner at Uncork Capital and invest broadly in information technology companies, with a particular focus on consumer, marketplace, and fintech companies. I also lead Uncork's later stage efforts. Previously, I was a General Partner at August Capital, a Senior Associate at Spectrum Equity, and started my career in the meat grinder as a fintech Analyst in investment banking. I led investments in ADARA, BARK, CommonBond, Compology, Hipcamp, Paperless Post, Quandl, Revel, Rocketmiles, SendBird, Spacious, Sun Basket, Wattpad, and Yumi, and was involved in investments in Amount, Avant, Ebates, Fastly , GitLab, Integral Ad Science , PubMatic, RJMetrics, Rockets of Awesome, Stitch Data, ThreatMetrix, and Zulily. My angel portfolio includes 100 Thieves, ALPEN, Alto IRA, Daring Foods, Fursure, neo.tax, and Saga. I went to Princeton University and I'm originally from the San Francisco Bay Area.

name: Tyler Churchill
about: Tyler is a Principal at Bonfire Ventures, a seed fund based in Los Angeles and focused on b2b software. Prior to joining Bonfire in 2019 he worked in sales at Everstring (acq. by ZoomInfo), a predictive analytics platform for sales and marketing, and TechValidate (acq. by SurveyMonkey), and customer marketing platform. He loves spending time working through the intersection of GTM + Product Strategy with founders, as well as running workshops on building narrative + effective messaging.

name: Vishal Harnal
about: Vishal leads 500ï¿½s investments across Southeast Asia through the 500 Durians Funds. He has invested in over 100 companies in the region in multiple sectors (consumer and enterprise internet; deep tech; consumer retail; blockchain) and is always on the hunt for talented, passionate founders. Vishal is also a Kauffman Fellow and teaches programs on venture capital at leading global universities. Prior to 500, Vishal was a career litigator specialized in resolving complex and high-stakes international investment disputes. He is making up for bad karma by building functional startup ecosystems in emerging markets.

name: Vivien Ho
about: Vivien is a Principal at Pear VC.

name: Wen-Wen Lam
about: Wen-Wen is a partner at Gradient Ventures. Previously, Wen-Wen was the CEO & Co-founder of NexTravel (YCW15), a leading corporate travel solution that serviced thousands of customers like Lyft, Twilio, and Stripe. She grew the business to over $100M in annual sales before exiting to Travelperk in 2020. Prior to this, Wen-Wen worked with a number of startups in leadership roles. She started her career in tech at LinkedIn. She received her BA from UC Berkeley in Economics and her MBA from USC Marshall School of Business.

name: William Leonard
about: William is an investor at Valor Ventures, a seed lead fund based in Atlanta where he focuses on next generation B2B fintech, Web3 infrastructure, digital health, and creator economy platforms in the Southeast US. Prior to joining Valor Ventures, William worked at VU Venture Partners and Dynamo Ventures as an investor focusing on early stage software across the globe.

name: Yuri Sagalov
about: Yuri is the founder and General Partner of Wayfinder Ventures, a pre-seed/seed fund based in the Bay Area. Prior to Wayfinder Yuri worked at Y Combinator for 5 years as a Partner, and has also personally seed invested in over 50 companies (including Flexport, Solugen, Boom Supersonic, Ajaib, Observe AI, Lively, NURX, and many more). Yuri was previously a founder and a software engineer.
